commands.review_summary:
The PR centralizes Codex synthetic usage selection, permits chat status usage without a saved OpenAI profile, and adds a conditional synthetic OpenAI usage snapshot to shared status output for configured Codex runtime routes.

PR surface: Source +79, Tests +158. Total +237 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: the released chat-status gate skips synthetic Codex usage when the route is usable but no local OAuth/token profile label exists, matching the linked v2026.6.6 report. The after-fix live command path has not been reproduced in the PR proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The implementation has no concrete automated repair finding; a maintainer should hold merge until the contributor supplies real end-to-end behavior proof.

target.title: Fix Codex synthetic usage in status without local OpenAI profiles
target.body:
## Summary
- route `/status` Codex synthetic usage through the Codex app-server marker even when no local OpenAI OAuth/token profile label exists
- teach shared `status --usage` to add the Codex synthetic OpenAI usage snapshot for configured OpenAI Codex runtime routes
- keep API-key/OpenClaw-pinned status contexts from probing Codex synthetic usage, and add regression coverage for no-profile and OpenClaw-pinned cases

Closes #92506

## Linked context
- Issue: #92506
- ClawSweeper intake: source-repro, queueable-fix, fix-shape-clear
- Upstream contract checked locally in sibling `../codex`: `account/rateLimits/read` maps to `GetAccountRateLimits` and returns Codex rate-limit snapshots used by the existing Codex provider usage hook

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: `/status` and shared status usage should request OpenAI usage through the Codex app-server synthetic auth route when an OpenAI model is effectively running on the Codex runtime, even if no local OpenAI profile label exists.
- Real environment tested: Windows local OpenClaw checkout at `C:\oc-work\openclaw-queue-worktrees\issue-92506-codex-status-usage`, Node with repo dependencies installed, branch head `220380b353`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node --import tsx -e "import { buildCodexSyntheticUsageAuth, shouldUseCodexSyntheticUsageForRuntime } from './src/status/codex-synthetic-usage.ts'; console.log(JSON.stringify({ behavior: 'codex synthetic usage route', noProfileCodexOpenAI: shouldUseCodexSyntheticUsageForRuntime({ provider: 'openai', effectiveHarness: 'codex' }), openClawPinned: shouldUseCodexSyntheticUsageForRuntime({ provider: 'openai', effectiveHarness: 'openclaw' }), auth: buildCodexSyntheticUsageAuth() }, null, 2));"
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
{
  "behavior": "codex synthetic usage route",
  "noProfileCodexOpenAI": true,
  "openClawPinned": false,
  "auth": {
    "provider": "openai",
    "token": "codex-app-server",
    "hookProvider": "codex"
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the changed status helper reports the no-profile OpenAI/Codex route as eligible, reports an OpenClaw-pinned OpenAI route as ineligible, and builds the exact synthetic auth payload that routes provider usage through the Codex hook while preserving the OpenAI provider context.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live Codex app-server account against a real OpenAI subscription in this workspace. The app-server request contract was checked against sibling `../codex`, and existing Codex provider tests cover parsing `account/rateLimits/read` responses.

## Supplemental tests and validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts src/commands/status-runtime-shared.test.ts src/commands/status-json.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.plugin.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.plugin.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/provider.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/rate-limits.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit --incremental false`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex -c service_tier='"fast"' review --base origin/main` -> no actionable regressions found

## Risk checklist
- No merge, land, or automerge performed
- No changelog added; scoped status/runtime bugfix with regression tests
- CI/checks: 135 success, 30 skipped, 1 neutral, 0 failures after the structured proof body update
- ClawSweeper review has started; final verdict pending
